ci.yml: no explicit permissions block — defaults to read-write (overly permissive)

firebase-hosting.yml: 1 third-party actions not pinned to SHA — vulnerable to supply chain attacks
firebase-hosting.yml: no explicit permissions block — defaults to read-write (overly permissive)
No engine constraints (engines in package.json or .nvmrc) — Node version not pinned
No SECURITY.md — users don't know how to report vulnerabilities
No CODEOWNERS file — no mandatory reviewers for critical paths
No CONTRIBUTING.md — onboarding is harder for new contributors
No pre-commit hooks (husky/lefthook) — lint/format not enforced before commit
No Dependabot/Renovate — dependency updates are manual and often forgotten
No linter config (ESLint/Biome/dart analyze) — code style not enforced
No formatter config (Prettier/Biome/.editorconfig) — inconsistent code formatting
TypeScript strict mode not enabled — allows implicit any and null errors
No test coverage configuration — coverage thresholds not enforced
No .dockerignore — node_modules and build artifacts will bloat Docker image
No commit convention enforcement (commitlint/changesets) — changelog generation is manual

assessment.patternDescription: Modular architecture — code organized by feature/responsibility.
insights: High cross-coupling (58%) — packages depend heavily on each other.
Stable foundation detected — core modules rarely change.
No god modules — healthy dependency distribution.

details.description: Detects contradictions between documentation and code. Finds stale README claims, incorrect JSDoc, outdated API docs, and misleading comments.
Detects internal contradictions: inconsistent validation, conflicting defaults, naming drift, dead config flags, and behavioral mismatches across modules.
